_owner: TechBrot Inc. — Certified QuickBooks ProAdvisor team
_methodology: One anonymized row is logged by the engaging ProAdvisor when a QuickBooks error or symptom engagement is resolved. Records the symptom, the QuickBooks product, the actual root cause found, whether self-fix had been attempted, who resolved it, and whether it recurred. No client-identifying data is ever stored. Observed only, never modeled. The published page reports the live N and never reports a statistic on a cell with N<20.

_schema.engagement_id: string — anonymous opaque id, no client linkage
_schema.primary_symptom: string — the presenting error code or symptom slug (e.g. h202, 6000-83, bank-feeds-not-working, reconciliation-broken)
_schema.qb_product: string — one of: desktop, premier, enterprise, online
_schema.root_cause_category: string — one of: network-hosting, file-damage, configuration, update-connection, payroll-subscription, browser, user-error, bank-aggregator, other
_schema.self_fix_attempted: boolean — had the client tried the documented self-fix before the engagement
_schema.resolution_hours: number — ProAdvisor hours to resolution (0 if self-fix sufficed)
_schema.recurring: boolean — had the same error recurred after a prior fix
